task:
ATOM 2 of the hybrid-plays priority (Play contract schema — METADATA ONLY). The founder accepted the ADR-0010 Play taxonomy in run_151 (see cocoder/SESSION_LOG.md top entry); the atom-2 gate is open. Your job: extend the Play data model with OPTIONAL, ADDITIVE, NON-BREAKING contract metadata that DERIVES ITS ALLOWED VALUES from the ADR-0010 taxonomy amendment, and prove existing prompt-only Plays still parse.

WHAT TO CHANGE:
1. packages/core/src/plays/types.ts — add optional fields to the `Play` interface (all `readonly` and `?`-optional so existing Plays remain valid). Cover the metadata named in the priority Objective atom 2, mapped onto the ADR-0010 axes:
   - executionModel?: 'prompt-only' | 'hybrid'   (ADR-0010 execution-model axis)
   - triggerClass?: 'lifecycle-triggered' | 'persona-requested' | 'tool/API-triggered'   (ADR-0010 trigger-class axis)
   - purpose?: string   (one-line purpose summary for the capability manifest)
   - allowedCallers?: readonly string[]   (lifecycle/persona/tool surfaces permitted to invoke)
   - inputSchema?: <a typed shape for the required input — your design; a structured object, not free text>
   - outputValidator?: <identifier/descriptor of the output contract validator — your design>
   - deterministicStep?: <descriptor of the optional deterministic precheck/gate for hybrid Plays — your design>
   - commitMode?: <the Play's commit behavior — your design, e.g. 'gated' | 'auto' | 'none'>
   - requiredCheckpoints?: readonly string[]   (e.g. the shared elegance checkpoint)
   plus any capability-manifest fields atom 4 will consume. Add the SAME optional fields to `PlayDelta` where it makes sense to override/extend. Add concise doc comments; the allowed string-literal unions for executionModel and triggerClass MUST match the ADR-0010 amendment's class names exactly — do not invent a parallel taxonomy or rename the classes.
2. packages/core/src/plays/loader.ts — parse the new optional frontmatter fields when present; when ABSENT, leave them undefined and NEVER throw. Validate enum-typed fields (executionModel/triggerClass/commitMode) only when present, with a clear error message naming the file and field, mirroring the existing asKind() style.

WHAT MUST NOT BREAK / BOUNDARY:
- This atom is SCHEMA + LOADER ONLY. Do NOT migrate any Play .md files (that is atom 3), do NOT generate the capability manifest (atom 4), do NOT touch dispatch.ts or invocation behavior (atom 7).
- Existing shipped prompt-only Plays (e.g. packages/personas/base/plays/wrap-up.md) declare only id/label/kind/writeScope today. They MUST still load with the new fields undefined. Additive and backward-compatible — no existing field changes meaning, no field becomes required.
- Match the surrounding code style (readonly interfaces, the loader's asString/asKind helpers).

WRITE SCOPE: packages/core/src/plays/** and packages/core/tests/plays*.test.ts ONLY.

TESTS (add to packages/core/tests/plays.test.ts or a sibling): prove (a) a Play .md with NONE of the new frontmatter fields parses successfully with every new field === undefined; (b) a Play .md declaring the new fields parses them with correct values; (c) an invalid enum value for executionModel or triggerClass throws a file+field-named error.

EXIT CRITERION (scripted, one-shot atom — not loop-amenable): `pnpm --dir 'packages/core' test` is green AND `pnpm --dir 'packages/core' exec tsc --noEmit` (or the repo's typecheck for this package) is green. Report the exact commands run and their exit codes, and the diff. Do not run git.

ATOM 3 of the hybrid-plays priority (migrate existing shipped Plays to the contract metadata). The atom-2 schema is committed (Play/PlayDelta now carry optional executionModel, triggerClass, purpose, allowedCallers, inputSchema, outputValidator, deterministicStep, commitMode, requiredCheckpoints; loader+merge parse them). No founder gate is pending. Your job: migrate every shipped base Play to the new contract metadata by editing its FRONTMATTER ONLY, classifying each honestly against the ADR-0010 taxonomy amendment.

FIRST, READ (do not classify from memory): (a) the ADR-0010 amendment 'Play taxonomy: trigger class × execution model' in cocoder/decisions/0010-taxonomy-and-authoring.md — it owns the class definitions and exact allowed values; (b) each Play's own body to judge what it actually does.

MIGRATE all 9 base Plays in packages/personas/base/plays/: archive-priority, code-review, create-priority, create-ticket, deep-read, documentation, edit-priority, electron-test, wrap-up. For EACH, add to its frontmatter:
   - executionModel: REQUIRED for every Play. Use 'prompt-only' unless the Play ALREADY has a real deterministic code spine; do NOT fabricate one (hybrid wiring is atoms 7-8). Prompt-only Plays must explicitly carry `executionModel: prompt-only` per the Objective.
   - triggerClass: REQUIRED for every Play — one of 'lifecycle-triggered' | 'persona-requested' | 'tool/API-triggered', chosen from how the Play is actually invoked (e.g. wrap-up is lifecycle-triggered; authoring Plays are persona-requested). Values must match the ADR-0010 amendment exactly.
   - purpose: REQUIRED — one honest line for the capability manifest.
   - allowedCallers: REQUIRED — the lifecycle/persona/tool surfaces that may invoke it, grounded in the persona/registry reality.
   - requiredCheckpoints: MANDATORY for the authoring + lifecycle-governance Plays — create-priority, edit-priority, archive-priority, create-ticket, and wrap-up MUST list the shared elegance checkpoint (Objective: authoring Plays enforce the shared elegance checkpoint before writing governance artifacts). Use a single consistent checkpoint string across them.
   - inputSchema / outputValidator / deterministicStep / commitMode: declare ONLY where genuinely true today; leave absent rather than inventing a dangling ref. (code-review/electron-test are the eventual hybrid candidates but their deterministic spines are atoms 7-8 — do not add them now.)

BOUNDARY / MUST NOT BREAK:
- FRONTMATTER METADATA ONLY. Do NOT rewrite any Play body/procedure text. Do NOT build the capability manifest (atom 4), the typed request lane (atom 5), the trigger registry (atom 6), or dispatch (atom 7).
- The loader must still parse all 9 Plays; every executionModel/triggerClass/commitMode value must be valid per the schema. Keep all existing fields (id/label/kind/writeScope/body) unchanged in meaning.

TESTS: add (or extend an existing test in packages/core/tests/) a MIGRATION-COMPLETENESS test that loads every shipped base Play and asserts (a) each declares a defined executionModel AND triggerClass, and (b) create-priority, edit-priority, archive-priority, create-ticket, and wrap-up each include the shared elegance checkpoint in requiredCheckpoints. This test is the durable guard that no shipped Play is left as un-migrated legacy markdown.

WRITE SCOPE: packages/personas/base/plays/** and packages/core/tests/** ONLY.

EXIT CRITERION (scripted, one-shot — not loop-amenable): `pnpm --dir 'packages/core' test` is green AND the canonical `pnpm typecheck` (root: tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.json) exits 0. Report the exact commands, their exit codes, and the diff. Do not run git.

ATOM 4 of the hybrid-plays priority (capability manifest). The Play contract metadata (atom 2) and the migrated base Plays (atom 3) are committed; `listEffectivePlays(sources)` in packages/core/src/plays/effective.ts returns the effective Play set with that metadata. Your job: generate a COMPACT per-persona 'Available Plays' manifest and include it in persona LAUNCH prompts — WITHOUT injecting any full Play body.

FIRST, READ: packages/core/src/runner/prompts.ts (the persona launch-prompt builders — buildOrchestratorPrompt/buildObserverPrompt and the builder/standby prompt), packages/core/src/plays/effective.ts (listEffectivePlays), packages/core/src/plays/dispatch.ts (note line ~104: the FULL body is injected ONLY at dispatch — that must stay the one place a body appears), and a couple of migrated Plays in packages/personas/base/plays/ to see real metadata values.

BUILD:
1. A pure renderer (new function in packages/core/src/plays/, exported via index.ts), e.g. `renderPlayManifest(plays: readonly Play[], caller: string): string`. It filters to Plays whose `allowedCallers` includes `caller`, and renders ONE compact line/block per Play containing: id, one-line purpose, trigger class, mandatory|optional, write behavior, and required input shape. It MUST NOT include the Play body. Rules:
   - mandatory|optional is a PURE PROJECTION of triggerClass: lifecycle-triggered => mandatory; persona-requested and tool/API-triggered => optional. Do NOT add a stored `mandatory` field or any new contract — the formal mandatory trigger registry is atom 6 and owns that concept; here you only DERIVE the label from the committed triggerClass. Centralize the rule in one tiny helper so atom 6 can later import it rather than re-deriving.
   - write behavior: derive from writeScope — 'read-only' when empty, otherwise a compact summary of the scope globs.
   - required input shape: from inputSchema when present (its ref), else 'none'.
2. Wire it into the LAUNCH prompts in prompts.ts: the orchestrator (Oscar), observer (Deb), and builder (Bob) launch prompts each gain an 'Available Plays' section built from the manifest. The runner (packages/core/src/runner/runner.ts) computes the effective Plays via listEffectivePlays and passes each persona its manifest (caller key: oscar/deb/bob). Keep the prompt builders pure — pass the manifest string in as input, the same way oscarBody/debBody/bobBody are passed.

BOUNDARY / MUST NOT BREAK:
- Do NOT inject any full Play body into launch prompts. dispatch.ts stays the ONLY place a body is rendered into a prompt; do not change dispatch.ts.
- Do NOT build the typed Play-request lane (atom 5), the mandatory trigger registry (atom 6), or hybrid deterministic execution (atom 7). Manifest generation + launch-prompt wiring ONLY.
- Preserve all existing prompt content and the existing prompts.test.ts expectations; the manifest is additive.

TESTS (extend packages/core/tests/prompts.test.ts and/or a new plays test):
   - the rendered manifest for a caller includes that Play's id, purpose, trigger class, and the correct mandatory|optional label, and lists ONLY Plays that caller may call (filtering proven: a Play whose allowedCallers excludes the caller is absent);
   - a lifecycle-triggered Play renders as 'mandatory' and a persona-requested Play renders as 'optional';
   - a built persona launch prompt CONTAINS the compact manifest (a Play id + purpose) and does NOT contain a known full-body phrase from that Play (prove bodies are not injected).

WRITE SCOPE: packages/core/src/plays/**, packages/core/src/runner/prompts.ts, packages/core/src/runner/runner.ts, and packages/core/tests/** ONLY.

EXIT CRITERION (scripted, one-shot — not loop-amenable): `pnpm --dir 'packages/core' test` is green AND the canonical `pnpm typecheck` (root: tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.json) exits 0. Report the exact commands, their exit codes, and the diff. Do not run git.

ATOM 5 of the hybrid-plays priority (typed Play request lane). Atoms 2-4 are committed: Play contract metadata, migrated base Plays, and the capability manifest (with `playAvailability(play)` deriving mandatory|optional from triggerClass, exported from packages/core/src/plays/index.js). Your job: define the TYPED PLAY-REQUEST contract and its VALIDATOR, so a persona requests an OPTIONAL Play through a structured handoff and the runner/daemon validates it BEFORE any dispatch. Personas must not execute Play procedures from memory.

FIRST, READ for idiom and reuse: packages/core/src/runner/directive.ts (the existing typed-handoff parser style — MalformedXError class, isRecord guard, throw-to-reject), packages/core/src/plays/manifest.ts (reuse playAvailability — do NOT re-derive mandatory/optional), packages/core/src/plays/effective.ts (listEffectivePlays / the Play catalog), and packages/core/src/plays/types.ts (Play contract fields).

BUILD a new module (e.g. packages/core/src/plays/request.ts, exported via packages/core/src/plays/index.ts and the package index):
1. A `PlayRequest` type and `parsePlayRequest(json: string): PlayRequest` that parses the structured handoff `{"kind":"play","play":"<id>","input":{...}}`. Mirror directive.ts: a dedicated error class, isRecord guards, clear messages; reject a missing/blank `play` id or a wrong `kind`. `input` is an optional object.
2. A `validatePlayRequest(request, { caller, plays })` (caller = requesting persona id; plays = the effective Play catalog) returning a typed result — accepted (with the resolved Play) or rejected (with a one-line reason). It enforces, each as a distinct rejection reason:
   - PLAY EXISTS: the requested id resolves in the catalog (else reject).
   - PERSONA AUTHORIZED: caller is in the Play's allowedCallers (else reject).
   - OPTIONAL LANE ONLY: the Play is optional per `playAvailability(play)` — REJECT any request for a `mandatory` (lifecycle-triggered) Play, because those are runner/daemon-owned (atom 6), never persona discretion. Reuse playAvailability; do not copy the rule.
   - INPUT CONTRACT: when the Play declares an inputSchema, the request must carry a non-empty `input` object (else reject). (inputSchema is a ref descriptor today; presence-check is sufficient — do not invent a JSON-schema engine.)
   - WRITE SCOPE: surface the resolved Play's writeScope as the dispatch boundary in the accepted result, so the eventual dispatcher commits within it.

BOUNDARY / SEAM (do NOT front-run later atoms):
- This atom is the REQUEST CONTRACT + VALIDATOR ONLY. Do NOT wire it into the live runner read-loop, do NOT actually dispatch the Play, do NOT touch dispatch.ts (hybrid deterministic execution is atom 7), and do NOT build the mandatory trigger registry (atom 6).
- Do NOT change the existing directive.ts delegate/wrapup contract.
- If PlayAssignment data is readily available and cleanly applicable, you MAY check it; if it would require new plumbing, leave assignment integration to atom 6/7 and note it in a code comment rather than inventing a parallel assignment store.

TESTS (new test file under packages/core/tests/): cover parse success + each parse rejection (bad kind, missing play id), and validatePlayRequest for the happy path AND each of the four validation rejections (unknown Play, unauthorized persona, mandatory/lifecycle Play rejected, missing required input). Assert the accepted result carries the resolved Play's writeScope.

WRITE SCOPE: packages/core/src/plays/**, packages/core/src/index.ts, and packages/core/tests/** ONLY.

EXIT CRITERION (scripted, one-shot — not loop-amenable): `pnpm --dir 'packages/core' test` is green AND the canonical `pnpm typecheck` (root: tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.json) exits 0. Report the exact commands, their exit codes, and the diff. Do not run git.

ATOM 6 of the hybrid-plays priority (mandatory trigger registry). Today the wrap-up Play is ALREADY runner-owned (runner.ts dispatches it on the wrapup directive, not Oscar), but WHICH Play fires at the wrap lifecycle point is a hardcoded magic-string 'wrap-up' duplicated in TWO assembly sites: packages/cli/src/run.ts (~line 166) and packages/daemon/src/launcher.ts (~line 117). Your job: introduce a declarative MANDATORY TRIGGER REGISTRY that becomes the SINGLE OWNER of lifecycle-point -> Play bindings, seed it with the ONE existing trigger (wrap-up), and route both assembly sites through it. Start with this one trigger and make the registry SHAPE ready for more; do NOT rewrite other dispatch paths.

FIRST, READ: packages/cli/src/run.ts and packages/daemon/src/launcher.ts (the two sites that hardcode 'wrap-up' and pass wrapPlay/wrapPlayAssignment into runRun), packages/core/src/runner/runner.ts around the wrapup branch (~line 1079) to confirm the runner already owns the dispatch, packages/core/src/plays/manifest.ts (reuse playAvailability), and packages/core/src/plays/effective.ts (loadEffectivePlay/listEffectivePlays).

BUILD (registry in packages/core/src/plays/, e.g. triggers.ts, exported via plays/index.ts and the package index):
1. A declarative registry of MANDATORY lifecycle/policy triggers: a typed mapping from a trigger point (seed exactly one: e.g. `run-wrap`) to the Play id the runner/daemon must invoke. Persona discretion is excluded by construction — these are runner/daemon-owned.
2. A resolver `resolveMandatoryPlay(triggerPoint, effectivePlays)` (or similar) that finds the bound Play in the effective catalog and ASSERTS it is mandatory via `playAvailability(play) === 'mandatory'` (i.e. lifecycle-triggered) — throw a clear error if a non-mandatory Play is ever bound to a mandatory trigger. Reuse playAvailability; do NOT re-derive the mandatory/optional rule.
3. Seed the registry: `run-wrap` -> `wrap-up`.
4. Route BOTH assembly sites through the registry: replace the hardcoded 'wrap-up' literal in packages/cli/src/run.ts AND packages/daemon/src/launcher.ts with a registry resolution, so there is ONE owner of the wrap-trigger binding. Behavior must stay IDENTICAL — the wrap-up Play is still loaded and dispatched at wrap with the same assignment/persona-mode and the same founder closeout-contract handling. Do not change runner.ts wrap-up behavior.

BOUNDARY / SEAM (do NOT front-run later atoms):
- ONLY the wrap-up trigger is wired this atom. The registry shape may anticipate ticket-close-on-success and authoring triggers, but do NOT wire their dispatch now (that is the 'rewrite all dispatch paths at once' the priority warns against).
- Do NOT touch dispatch.ts / hybrid deterministic execution (atom 7) and do NOT change the typed request lane (atom 5).
- Preserve the founder closeout contract path exactly; all existing wrap-up tests must stay green.

TESTS: registry resolver returns the wrap-up Play for the run-wrap trigger from a catalog; resolver THROWS when a non-mandatory (e.g. persona-requested) Play is bound to a mandatory trigger; and the existing cli/daemon/runner wrap-up tests still pass unchanged.

WRITE SCOPE: packages/core/src/plays/**, packages/core/src/index.ts, packages/cli/src/run.ts, packages/daemon/src/launcher.ts, and packages/{core,cli,daemon}/tests/** ONLY.

EXIT CRITERION (scripted, one-shot — not loop-amenable): `pnpm -r test` is green (all packages, since cli+daemon change) AND the canonical `pnpm typecheck` (root: tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.json) exits 0. Report the exact commands, their exit codes, and the diff. Do not run git.

ATOM 7 of the hybrid-plays priority (hybrid deterministic execution in dispatchPlay). The Play schema (atom 2) already carries an optional `deterministicStep?: { ref: string }`. Your job: make `dispatchPlay` (packages/core/src/plays/dispatch.ts) run that OPTIONAL deterministic step BEFORE the LLM invocation, capture its structured result, and either GATE or FEED the LLM layer — staying ONE-LEVEL (the deterministic step is a plain captured subprocess, NEVER a recursive Play dispatch).

FIRST, READ: packages/core/src/plays/dispatch.ts (current dispatchPlay — note runHeadlessProcess as the existing injectable subprocess seam via DispatchPlayDeps.runHeadless, and how the LLM prompt is built at line ~104 then run via the adapter), and packages/core/src/plays/types.ts (the deterministicStep field).

BUILD:
1. Add an INJECTABLE deterministic-step seam to DispatchPlayDeps, mirroring the existing runHeadless pattern: e.g. `runDeterministic?: (input: { ref: string; cwd: string; timeoutMs?: number; signal?: AbortSignal }) => Promise<{ ok: boolean; output: string }>`. Default implementation: run the step's `ref` as a captured subprocess (reuse runHeadlessProcess), with ok === (exitCode === 0). Keep the default minimal and documented; the precise ref->command convention and a real script land in atom 8 — do NOT over-build ref resolution here.
2. In dispatchPlay, when `input.play.deterministicStep` is present (a hybrid Play):
   - Run the deterministic step first, capturing { ok, output }.
   - GATE: if NOT ok, do NOT build or run the LLM/adapter invocation at all; return a result that signals the gate (e.g. a non-zero exitCode and the deterministic output), so a failed precheck demonstrably blocks the LLM layer.
   - FEED: if ok, inject the captured deterministic output into the LLM prompt (e.g. a clearly-labeled '## Deterministic precheck result' section appended to the existing body+task prompt), so the LLM layer is demonstrably driven by the deterministic result.
   - When `deterministicStep` is ABSENT, behavior is IDENTICAL to today (no deterministic call; prompt and run unchanged).
3. Make the gate observable: extend DispatchPlayResult ADDITIVELY with an optional field (e.g. `deterministic?: { ok: boolean; output: string }` and/or `gated?: boolean`) so callers/tests can see the precheck result and whether the LLM was gated. Keep it optional so existing callers (runner.ts wrap-up path) are unaffected.

BOUNDARY / SEAM (do NOT front-run atom 8):
- ONE-LEVEL ONLY: the deterministic step is a subprocess/command, never a nested dispatchPlay or Play delegation.
- Do NOT migrate or author a real hybrid Play, and do NOT add a real scripts/* deterministic script — that is atom 8's end-to-end proof. This atom delivers the dispatchPlay capability + its unit tests using an INJECTED runDeterministic.
- Do NOT change the typed request lane (atom 5) or the trigger registry (atom 6).
- Preserve all existing dispatchPlay behavior for non-hybrid Plays; existing dispatch tests must stay green.

TESTS (extend packages/core/tests/plays-dispatch.test.ts), all using an injected runDeterministic and injected runHeadless/adapter so no real CLI spawns:
   - hybrid Play, precheck OK -> the LLM invocation RUNS and the prompt passed to the adapter CONTAINS the deterministic output (feed proven);
   - hybrid Play, precheck NOT ok -> the LLM/adapter invocation is NOT called (gate proven) and the result reflects the gate (gated/non-zero, deterministic output present);
   - non-hybrid Play (no deterministicStep) -> runDeterministic is never called and behavior is unchanged.

WRITE SCOPE: packages/core/src/plays/** and packages/core/tests/** ONLY.

EXIT CRITERION (scripted, one-shot — not loop-amenable): `pnpm --dir 'packages/core' test` is green AND the canonical `pnpm typecheck` (root: tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.json) exits 0. Report the exact commands, their exit codes, and the diff. Do not run git.
